ksa_text: Develop and maintain operational documentation for data and AI systems, including standard operating procedures, runbooks, system security documentation, knowledge base articles, technical documentation, and process diagrams.

dcwf.top_element_label: Develop system testing and validation procedures, programming, and documentation.

nice.top_element_label: Develop data standards, policies, and procedures

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Operations & Program Management is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Master Data Management. This task KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Operations & Program Management), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Framework signal is weak (composite 0.43). External validation of this task KSA for the Director of Master Data Management role relies primarily on domain-level alignment rather than specific framework matches.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: PMI, TOGAF operational frameworks, and CDO survey data (NewVantage Partners) confirm operational program management as a key competency for data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: domain-level relevance of Operations & Program Management for the Director of Master Data Management is corroborated by DAMA-DMBOK, CDO survey data, and role-specific job market analysis, despite weak cross-framework signal.

decision_rationale: Operations & Program Management task KSA is within scope for the Director of Master Data Management at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
